allowed_claim: CFD is the current high-fidelity reference used to construct and test reduced-model evidence.
forbidden_claim: CFD validates the thesis model experimentally.

allowed_claim: The thesis contribution is a provenance-controlled CFD-to-1D closure/admission workflow and evidence map.
forbidden_claim: The thesis contribution is one final tuned Nu f_D or K coefficient.
caveat: Negative and blocked results remain part of the contribution.

allowed_claim: The target architecture is steady fluid+walls with pressure and thermal lanes plus segment-local metadata.
forbidden_claim: The architecture itself proves final predictive accuracy.
caveat: Different model slots have different admission states.

allowed_claim: Predictive runtime modes must use setup-known inputs and cannot use protected CFD or validation outputs.
forbidden_claim: CFD mdot wallHeatFlux cooler duty validation temperatures holdout rows or external tests can be hidden predictive inputs.
caveat: This rule must appear anywhere predictive claims are discussed.

allowed_claim: The final split separates training rows training-support perturbations holdout/testing rows and external test.
forbidden_claim: Rows can be freely interchanged for tuning selection and scoring.
caveat: External-test use depends on matching heat-loss/admission package.

allowed_claim: Internal Nu must not absorb heat-loss radiation cooler passive wall heater source-placement or residual errors.
forbidden_claim: A single internal Nu can be fitted from total residual without owner separation.
caveat: Use residual-owner language until admitted thermal closures exist.

allowed_claim: Radiation and external heat-loss effects may be embedded in realized CFD wall BC quantities and must not be double counted.
forbidden_claim: Realized wallHeatFlux can be used both as a diagnostic target and predictive runtime source.
caveat: The authoritative CFD wall setup is `0/T`, not a post-hoc assumption.

allowed_claim: Time-window uncertainty is a separate evidence label from model-form error.
forbidden_claim: Time-window scatter is the full model uncertainty.
caveat: Same-QOI UQ is required before admission claims.

allowed_claim: Mesh/GCI status is row-scoped and must be labeled when using CFD reductions.
forbidden_claim: Any one mesh statement globally certifies all CFD-derived closures.
caveat: Keep mesh caveats compact but explicit.

allowed_claim: Property lane and literature source envelope must be identified before closure fitting or prose generalization.
forbidden_claim: A literature equation can be imported as an admitted TAMU closure without source matching.

allowed_claim: Validation temperatures and sensor QoIs are targets or scoring outputs, not predictive inputs.
forbidden_claim: Validation temperatures can be supplied to the runtime model as boundary conditions for predictive claims.
caveat: Applies to train holdout and external-test scoring.

allowed_claim: The work is relevant to SAM/CSEM because it provides a vetted closure/admission workflow and branchwise evidence map.
forbidden_claim: The thesis validates SAM or claims SAM-ready closure transfer.
caveat: Keep SAM language interpretive and forward-looking.

allowed_claim: When isolation and recovery are not demonstrated pressure evidence should be section/cluster/effective/residual rather than component K.
forbidden_claim: A pressure rise or basis-mismatched diagnostic is an admitted negative-loss component coefficient.
caveat: Supports negative result and pressure-basis ladder.

allowed_claim: The current S14/F6 pressure path admits zero coefficient and should be reported as non-admission.
forbidden_claim: A non-admitted F6/corner coefficient is ready for predictive runtime use.
caveat: Detailed result prose belongs in Ch7/Ch8 packet.
